title: Metastelma woodsonii
taxonomy: Metastelma woodsonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a type of twining vine native to Mexico. The classification of Metastelma woodsonii places it within the genus Metastelma, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "Mexican bugleweeds" due to their distinctive shape. Metastelma woodsonii is closely related to other species within the Apocynaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes milkweeds and oleander. The exact classification of Metastelma woodsonii can be found in various botanical resources, including the Kew Royal Botanic Gardens' Plants of the World Online database.

intro: Metastelma woodsonii, commonly known as Woodson's Metastelma, is a type of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Metastelma woodsonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties. Metastelma woodsonii is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being incorporated into various herbal supplements and tonics.
